page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8581C processor cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with the instance achieving top-tier status in Geekbench single-core testing and single-threaded data compression tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core scaling and memory-intensive operations show clear limitations. Multi-core performance in stress-ng and software compilation with clang fall into the lower percentiles. Uncached large-block memory read bandwidth and the PassMark memory score are weak, reflecting the constraints of the 8.0 GB memory allocation.

Given its hardware profile, the c4-highcpu-4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for single-threaded compute tasks, text processing, and lightweight web serving that do not demand large memory footprints. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, Redis key-value operations, and basic single-threaded data processing. Conversely, it is poorly suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale multi-threaded software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference, where text generation speeds drop significantly.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 2 physical cores and hyper-threading, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, particularly in single-threaded compression and text processing benchmarks. However, its multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in multi-threaded decompression and uncached memory reads. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for single-threaded compute tasks, static web serving, and lightweight Redis operations, while rendering it less suitable for memory-heavy databases or complex multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight, compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, ideal for single-threaded tasks.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine delivering strong single-core performance and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for lightweight, compute-focused workloads.

Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core
